channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Muslim leaders call for calm and respectful political discourse between Ruto and opposition
item.description: Muslim leaders in Kenya have called on President William Ruto and opposition figures to reduce the level of abusive and confrontational language in their political engagements. Their appeal comes at a...

item.title: Delhi NCR Experiences Fog and Rainy Spells Over Three Days
item.description: Delhi and the surrounding NCR region witnessed an unusual weather pattern this week, with rare dense fog appearing alongside intermittent spells of rain and thunderstorms over a period of three days. ...

item.title: Florida man clarifies identity after viral video sparks mistaken claims he is Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: A Florida man known online as “Palm Beach Pete” has gone viral after a video showing him driving a convertible in South Florida led some viewers to speculate that he resembled the late financier Jeffr...

item.title: Editorial Calls for Resolution of Cricket Kenya's Leadership Crisis
item.description: The editorial in Nation.Africa passionately calls for an end to the ongoing chaos plaguing Cricket Kenya, the body governing cricket in our country. It highlights years of infighting, poor governance,...

item.title: Nigerian Breweries Introduces Big Fiesta, Nationwide Cultural and Music Events
item.description: As Naija dey ginger up for the next celebration season, Nigerian Breweries Plc don officially launch Big Fiesta, a new cultural platform wey go gather people across the country through music, social e...

item.title: Government Assistance Programs for Repatriated Overseas Filipino Workers
item.description: The Philippine government, through the Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) and other agencies like OWWA and DSWD, offers comprehensive support to repatriated Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) especiall...

item.title: Ateneo Blue Eagles Edge Out UST Golden Spikers in Five-Set Thriller to Claim Solo Fourth in UAAP Season 88 Men's Volleyball
item.description: In a gripping five-set showdown during UAAP Season 88 men's volleyball on March 21, 2026, the Ateneo Blue Eagles pulled off a hard-fought victory over the University of Santo Tomas Golden Spikers at t...

item.title: Alberta Government Announces Closure of Supervised Consumption Sites in Calgary and Lethbridge by June End
item.description: The Alberta government announced on March 20, 2026, that the supervised consumption sites (SCS) in Calgary and Lethbridge will close at the end of June. Calgary's site at the Sheldon M. Chumir Health ...

item.title: Trump Administration Issues 30-Day Sanctions Waiver for Iranian Oil Sales at Sea to Ease Global Price Pressures Amid Ongoing War
item.description: The Trump administration announced a 30-day waiver on sanctions, allowing the purchase and sale of Iranian oil already loaded on vessels at sea. This move, effective until April 19, 2026, aims to rele...

item.title: Wealth Tax as a Moral Response to Inequality in the Philippines
item.description: Tinalakay sa artikulo ang argumento na ang pagpapatupad ng wealth tax sa Pilipinas ay isang moral na obligasyon sa harap ng matinding hindi pagkakapantay-pantay sa lipunan. Binanggit ang pananaw ng ec...

item.title: Hidden financial risks add complexity to South Africa’s just energy transition funding, says Ramokgopa
item.description: Electricity and Energy Minister Kgosientsho Ramokgopa has highlighted that so-called “hidden risks” are making it more difficult to properly assess and utilise funding linked to South Africa’s Just En...

item.title: Rep. Jim Himes Lobbies Democrats to Renew FBI's Warrantless Section 702 Surveillance Authority Amid Oversight Concerns
item.description: Congressman Jim Himes, the ranking Democrat on the House Intelligence Committee, is privately urging fellow Democrats to support the renewal of Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act...

item.title: Sales of prime ministerial Toby Jugs reveal Keir Starmer topped 2025 figures while Boris Johnson remains most popular over five years
item.description: According to sales data from the Houses of Parliament gift shop, Sir Keir Starmer has emerged as the best-selling prime minister in Toby Jug form for 2025, with 116 units sold, including 32 purchased ...

item.title: MEA confirms six Indian nationals killed in escalating West Asia conflict, with first fatality reported in Saudi Arabia
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) has confirmed that the total number of Indian nationals killed in the ongoing West Asia conflict has reached six. The latest casualty is an Indian national who d...

item.title: Isuzu East Africa Concludes Longtime Partnership With Associated Motors After 50 Years
item.description: Isuzu East Africa has officially ended its dealership partnership with Associated Motors Limited, bringing to a close a working relationship that has lasted nearly 50 years. The company made the annou...

item.title: Karachi police arrest six for running fake call centre scamming foreigners
item.description: The National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) in Karachi has apprehended six individuals accused of operating a fake call centre that targeted foreign nationals through online scams. According...

item.title: Police bodycam footage reveals details of Justin Timberlake’s 2024 DUI-related traffic stop
item.description: Newly released police body camera footage provides a detailed look at a June 2024 traffic stop involving singer Justin Timberlake in Sag Harbor, New York. Officers pulled Timberlake over after observi...

item.title: Quebec Court Temporarily Bans Two Men from Donating Sperm After Alleged High Number of Offspring
item.description: Quebec's Superior Court has issued a temporary injunction preventing a man and his son from donating sperm after they were accused of fathering an unusually high number of children. The legal action c...

item.title: Concerns over Kenya’s premature AI regulation amid sector growth
item.description: Kenya is considering the Artificial Intelligence Bill, 2026, which aims to regulate AI by creating an AI Commissioner, an AI Authority, and an Advisory Council. The Bill also criminalises deepfakes, s...

item.title: Collapsed transmission pylon leaves parts of Gqeberha without power for three weeks
item.description: A transmission pylon collapse in Bushy Park, Gqeberha, has left suburbs such as Summerstrand and Walmer without electricity, with repairs expected to take about 21 days. The incident occurred on 18 Ma...
